content.method: Structured cost-modeling exercise using administrative data on teacher workforce by subject/region, school capacity and enrollment projections, combined with scenario analysis calibrated against the Polish 1999/2017 transition case studies and Finland's multi-decade phase-in timeline.
content.question: What are the realistic transition costs (teacher displacement, school closures/mergers, cohort overlap, curriculum revision) of restructuring Hungary's tracking system, and how do these costs differ between full consolidation, partial phase-down of only the age-10 entry point, and status-quo-with-monitoring?
content.why_it_matters: Poland's 2017 reversal required closing ~7,000 schools and displaced up to 10,000 teachers; Hungary already faces documented teacher shortages and an aging rural workforce. Without credible cost estimates disaggregated by reform scope, policymakers cannot compare the delivery feasibility of TP-live-t2 versus the lower-risk TP-live-t5, or judge whether current implementation capacity supports any structural change.
